entry.title: Understanding SAP HANA Smart Data Access (SDA): Execution Boundaries and Remote Data Processing
entry.content:
Introduction
SAP HANA Smart Data Access (SDA) enables SAP HANA to access data stored in external systems without replicating the data into SAP HANA.

External systems are connected as remote sources, and their objects can be represented inside SAP HANA as virtual tables.
From an application perspective, these objects can be queried using standard SQL statements, while the data remains in the source system and is accessed during statement execution.

Official SAP HANA Smart Data Access documentation:
Data Access in the SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database

This article explains general processing behavior and supports correct interpretation during troubleshooting.
It does not provide configuration or performance tuning guidance.


Remote Sources and Virtual Tables
With SDA, SAP HANA integrates remote data access into SQL processing.
When a query references a virtual table, SAP HANA processes the statement, and data access is performed via the connected remote source during execution.
This behavior is part of the documented SAP HANA Smart Data Access architecture.


Query Processing Context
In SAP HANA Smart Data Access, SQL statements referencing virtual tables may involve both local SAP HANA processing and operations executed against the connected remote source.
As part of query processing, operations may be executed locally in SAP HANA or against the connected remote source.
Query execution may therefore include processing steps across system boundaries as part of normal system behavior.


Support-Relevant Distinction
From a support perspective, the following distinction is essential:
A statement can be initiated and processed by SAP HANA, while parts of the effective data processing may take place outside SAP HANA via the remote source and the communication layer.
Because of this execution boundary, the status of SAP HANA services alone does not describe the complete processing path when Smart Data Access is used.


Observations in Troubleshooting
During troubleshooting, it is possible that local SAP HANA processing appears normal while statements accessing virtual tables show different response times.

This can occur because the execution path may include:

  • the SAP HANA database
  • the network communication layer
  • the connected remote source

The overall response time therefore reflects the complete end-to-end processing path.


Documented SDA Scenarios
SAP documentation and SAP Notes describe different SDA-related scenarios that can influence behavior during execution.

SDA FAQ:
https://me.sap.com/notes/2180119

Examples include:

  • Remote source object discovery error scenario
    https://me.sap.com/notes/2572731
  • SDA remote source connection test / communication error scenario
    https://me.sap.com/notes/3470273
  • Remote source object access / closed connection scenario
    https://me.sap.com/notes/2557181

These scenarios may occur while the SAP HANA database itself remains available.


Interpreting Monitoring
When remote sources are involved, troubleshooting should consider the full end-to-end data access path rather than only local database activity.


Support Scope Clarification
When analyzing statements accessing virtual tables, the involved components may include:

  • SAP HANA database
  • network communication
  • remote data source

Observed behavior alone is not sufficient to determine a root cause and requires end-to-end analysis across all involved components.


Conclusion
SAP HANA Smart Data Access allows access to external data without replication.
Virtual tables represent remote objects, and data access from the connected system is part of normal execution behavior.
Statements executed in SAP HANA may therefore include processing steps outside the SAP HANA database, while SAP HANA operates according to its documented architecture.
Understanding this behavior supports correct interpretation during troubleshooting.


Disclaimer
This article summarizes documented system behavior based on publicly available SAP documentation and SAP Knowledge Base Articles.

Only official SAP documentation and SAP Notes are authoritative.

Hello @stdo

You don't seem to be on Public Cloud.

Displaying the supplier IBAN/BIC in that popup would not be solved through standard S4H Cloud supplier master configuration.

Adding custom fields in Ariba Invoicing may allow additional invoice fields, but those fields appear to be input or invoice data fields, not automatically derived supplier master fields.

If your requirement is for invoice validation, the better route would be to raise this as an SAP Ariba enhancement request or check with the support team whether a customer-specific Ariba extension or integration option is available.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Private Cloud] Flexible Workflow – Sales Ord...
entry.content:
Given your constraints that pricing not persisted before commit, SELECT forbidden, the practical recommendation is

  • Stick with your current “launch always, evaluate later” design.
  • Add a final auto-release step in the workflow definition that runs if no approval step was executed.
  • This way, the workflow itself cleans up the status, and you don’t need to hack BAdIs to reset approval.

Having said the above, your next step should be

  • In Manage Workflows for Sales Orders, add a final step with action = releaseSalesOrder.
  • Condition: “NOT ZP06” (your custom evaluator).
  • Test:
    • With ZP06 → approval step runs, release after approval.
    • Without ZP06 → approval skipped, final step releases order.

entry.title: Re: Role Management Strategy in EWM Implementation Projec...
entry.content:
The recommended pattern is

  1. Build roles in DEV → Transport to QA for testing → Transport to PROD for consistency.
  2. Assign only the required roles in PROD; keep others unassigned until needed.
  3. Document role ownership and usage so that during audits or refreshes, you know exactly what’s safe to keep or remove.
  4. Use composite roles or business role concepts in EWM to group module-specific roles for easier assignment.

entry.title: Re: When user places cursor on Cost Center / Profit Cente...
entry.content:
Good day @S002476221520 

Thank you for your question.

You cannot configure the hover description for Cost Center/Profit Center and the additional Employee Number column in the Post Cash Journal Entries app.

The app does not currently support adding custom fields through the Custom Fields app. Therefore, an you cannot add Employee Number column as a custom column to the cash journal entry screen. For reconciliation, I recommend you use an existing available field such as Text, Assignment, or Supplier/Business Partner where you can.

I suggest you consider the following:

  1. Check whether Cost Center and Profit Center descriptions and translation are correctly maintained in Manage Cost Centers and Manage Profit Centers.                                                                                   
  2. Test the value help in Post Cash Journal Entries to confirm whether the descriptions are visible there.
  3. Use an existing standard reference field such as Text or Assignment.
  4. If you require a dedicated Employee Number column, you may raise or vote for a product improvement request.

Here are references for more guidance

3689593 - No Cost Center can be selected in app Post General Journal Entries - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

3730921 - Cannot add custom fields in the Post Cash Journal Entries - FBCJ app - SAP for Me

3283758 - Error TK569 in app 'Post Cash Journal Entries' while creating new entries. - SAP for Me

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: PR workflow is restarting unexpectedly
entry.content:
Yes, this is expected standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. Any change to the work order dates updates the linked purchase requisition header/item, which automatically re‑evaluates the workflow start conditions; even  your conditions are only based on price, currency, or quantity. There is no delivered way to exclude date changes from triggering the workflow; the only option is to handle it via custom logic.

Work with your functional consultant to implement a BAdI in Custom Fields and logic that checks the change type. If only dates are modified, bypass workflow trigger or auto‑release the PR. This is the only way to prevent unnecessary approvals in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Fiori App for Goods Receipt that Supports GOITEM-ANZG...
entry.content:
There is currently no standard SAP Fiori Goods Receipt app that exposes the field GOITEM-ANZGEB (“Number of Containers”). In Fiori apps like Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843) or Post Goods Movement, this field is not available. If you need to capture container counts during GR posting, you need to use MIGO in GUI or extend a Fiori app via custom development.

If Fiori adoption is mandatory, work with your development team to:

  • extend the CDS view for GR posting with GOITEM-ANZGEB.
  • add the field to the Fiori app via UI annotations.
  • implement validation logic in a BAdI to ensure the value is captured correctly.

This way, you retain the process integrity while moving users fully to Fiori.

entry.title: Re: Recipient Business System ID in outbound SOAP message...
entry.content:
Good day @dlindsey 

Thank you for your question.

The Recipient Business System ID for SAP_COM_0223 is not maintained directly as a separate editable field in the Communication Arrangements app. The communication arrangement uses the assigned system, and the relevant receiver related information is derived from that communication system setup.

So, in your case, the first place you should check is the Communication System assigned to the SAP_COM_0223 communication arrangement. See KBA 3530136 for requirements.

This basically means the following:

  • You cannot maintain Recipient Business System ID as a separate setting inside the SAP_COM_0223 communication arrangement.
  • The communication arrangement must point to the correct communication system.
  • The Business System information must be checked in the assigned communication system.
  • The customer or BP assignment in the communication system is also important for SAP_COM_0223 outbound routing.
  • If the outbound message is not triggered, or no message reaches Cloud Integration, the issue is usually linked to the communication system and business partner assignment rather than a missing field in the communication arrangement. See KBA 3618874 for more details on BP assignment.

In summary, the answer is: No, this is not set directly in the communication arrangement. You should check and maintain it through the assigned communication system.

A quick guide to consider:

In your Communications Arrangement, open the relevant communication arrangement, in the Common Data section, check which Communication System is assigned.

In Communication Systems app, search for the same communication system. Check the maintained system details, especially the System ID related value. Check the BP section and add the relevant customer or invoice recipient BP if you required specific routing. Save and retest both the communication system and the outbound message.

If your integration is intended to send all SAP_COM_0223 messages to one central middleware endpoint, then review whether the communication system should be maintained as a hub system. You can also reference KBA 3618874 here.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: CAP Node.js: Add authorization filter without breaking OData options
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m working with SAP CAP (Node.js) exposing OData services consumed by a SAPUI5 SmartTable.

I have a data model with a many-to-many relationship:

  • A MainEntity can have N SubEntities
  • A SubEntity can belong to M MainEntities

Example (simplified):

entity MainEntity {
  key ID : UUID;
  name   : String;
  subs   : Composition of many MainSub on subs.main = $self;
}

entity MainSub {
  key ID   : UUID;
  main     : Association to MainEntity;
  subCode  : String;
}

 Requirement

I need to implement row-level authorization in the backend such that:

A user can only see MainEntities that are linked to SubEntities assigned to that user.

Conceptually:

WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM MainSub s
  WHERE s.main_ID = MainEntity.ID
    AND s.subCode IN (userAllowedSubs)
)

Problem:

The frontend (SmartTable) sends dynamic OData queries like:

$filter=contains(name,'ABC')
&$orderby=name asc
&$top=100
&$skip=0

If I override the query in a before READ handler like:

req.query.SELECT.where = ...

 I break the original OData query, losing filtering, sorting, paging, etc.

I’m looking for the correct CAP pattern to:

  • Inject an authorization restriction (AND condition)
  • Preserve all incoming OData query options:
    • $filter
    • $orderby
    • $top / $skip
    • $select
  • Work correctly with SAPUI5 SmartTable

Expected behavior

Final query should behave like:

WHERE
  <original OData filter>
  AND EXISTS (authorization condition)

Additional context

This is conceptually similar to row-level authorization in SAP ERP (e.g. restricting data visibility based on organizational assignments), but implemented in CAP.

Questions

  1. What is the recommended way in CAP to append conditions to the existing CQN instead of replacing it?
  2. Is using req.query.where(...) with EXISTS the correct approach?
  3. Are there better patterns (e.g. CDS views, aspects, annotations) for this type of authorization?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!!

entry.title: Unable to assign zero value to dummy SKU for shipping-only refund (S/4HANA Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi team,

We are integrating Shopify with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and using the Credit Memo Request (A2X API) to process refunds. Our requirement is to refund only shipping charges, without refunding the product. Based on earlier guidance, we created a dummy material (SKU) to represent the refund item.

However, we are facing an issue where we are not able to assign a value of 0.00 to the dummy SKU (base price condition PPR0). The system requires a positive value (e.g., 0.01).

Our questions are:

  1. Why is a dummy SKU required instead of using the original material from the invoice?
  2. Is there a supported way to allow zero-value pricing for this dummy item?
  3. If a separate pricing procedure is required for refunds, how can it be assigned in Public Cloud given that new document types cannot be created?

Any guidance or best practices for this scenario would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to assign zero value to dummy SKU for shipping...
entry.content:
Hi,

This is a common scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition when handling shipping-only refunds via Credit Memo Request (A2X API).

1. Why use a dummy SKU?

In S/4HANA Cloud, a credit memo request must contain at least one item for pricing to run.

  • If you use the original material, the system copies product pricing, taxes, and accounting logic, which may result in an unintended product refund.
  • A dummy material (e.g., SHIP-REFUND) is used as a technical placeholder so that only the freight/refund condition determines the credit value.

 

2. Why zero value is not allowed

The base price condition (e.g., PPR0) in standard pricing procedures is usually non-statistical, meaning it contributes to the net value.

Because of this, the system does not allow a value of 0.00 and expects a valid price.

 

3. Supported solution

The recommended approach in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is to use a dedicated pricing setup for refund scenarios:

    1. Create a separate pricing procedure for refunds
      • Copy the existing pricing procedure used for standard sales.
      • In the copied procedure, set the base price condition (e.g., PPR0/PR00) as Statistical so it does not contribute to the net value.
      • Ensure the refund-relevant condition (e.g., freight or custom refund condition) remains active and determines the credit amount.
    2. Use a technical (dummy) material for refund items
      • Create a material that acts as a placeholder item in the credit memo request.
      • This item carries pricing but does not represent an actual product.
    3. Assign the refund pricing procedure via Pricing Procedure Determination
      • Use SSCUI “Set Pricing Procedure Determination” to assign the new pricing procedure.
      • Differentiate the refund scenario using a combination such as:
        • Sales Organization
        • Distribution Channel
        • Document Pricing Procedure
        • Customer Pricing Procedure
      • For example, assign a dedicated Customer Pricing Procedure to refund scenarios and map it to the new pricing procedure.

This is a standard and supported design pattern in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for refund-only integrations.

Hope this helps!

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud] Regarding the assembly and consumptio...
entry.content:
Root Cause

When the component was HALB, its PIR was consumed because it was treated as a dependent requirement from the parent.

After changing it to FERT, it became an independent finished product, so:
👉 Its PIR is only consumed by its own sales orders, not by the parent material demand.

Option 1 (Best & Recommended): Use Phantom Assembly

  • Set Special Procurement Key = 50 (Phantom Assembly) for the FERT component.
  • This makes the system treat it like a dependent component.

For More Visit: KableOne

entry.title: Re: Requirement for a solution to Mass Update Asset Maste...
entry.content:
Hello @K12 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your screenshot and requirement, it is a scenario where you must correct the fixed asset master data. The field UL in Years in the Valuation tab of the Manage Fixed Assets app represents the planned useful life maintained per depreciation area. In your case, the incorrect value was uploaded during migration and now needs to be corrected for around 2000 assets.

The key point is that you should not try to solve this by reusing the Migrate Your Data app for assets you have already migrated. That is intended only for initial data migration. Since you have existing fixed asset master data, you should follow the mass correction route and use the released fixed asset APIs or, where available in your activated scope, the Mass Processing of Assets app.

Here are references for more guidance

3625447 - How to mass change Fixed Asset in S/4HANA Cloud Public edition? - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

3219541 - Mass create Fixed assets - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

So, in simple terms:

  • Yes, you can fulfil the requirement at mass scale, but normally through released fixed asset master data APIs. Not by manually changing each asset.
  • You should focus on the planned useful life field in the relevant depreciation area, in your example is it Depreciation Area 15.
  • If the Mass Processing of Assets app is available in your system, check whether it supports the required validation field change for your scope. If it is not available, use the released Fixed Asset Master Data API option.
  • A custom upload button inside Manage Fixed Assets is not the standard approach.
  • Before you update 2000 assets, first test with one or two assets and check the recalculation impact on depreciation.

Please, also check the configuration side before you change the asset values. The following references provide good insights.

3701242 - Planned useful life and expired useful life cannot remain "0" when using the migration cockpit to migrate asset master data - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

3587697 - Error FAA_MD026 when releasing an Enterprise Project - SAP for Me

For your case, the clean approach I would recommend is:

  1. Confirm whether the wrong useful life value is only in Depreciation Area 15.
  2. Confirm whether the field is manually editable for one test asset.
  3. Check whether the value is being derived from asset class configuration or was simply uploaded incorrectly.
  4. If it is only wrong master data, mass update the field using the released fixed asset API.
  5. If the value is being redetermined from asset class configuration, correct the configuration first and then test the asset behaviour again.

See the following references for more details.

3048885 - How to change the expired useful life of an asset - S/4HANA Cloud Public edition - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/5bcca49998834de2b5533c36602e52a2/560ade3edf964466b10cc918c50cfa8e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/5bcca49998834de2b5533c36602e52a2/90f53e2fff42443ea10dd9d1a7605df4.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: É um prazer fazer parte desta comunidade SAP
entry.content:
Olá, pessoal! É um prazer fazer parte desta comunidade SAP.

Meu nome é Criste Jones, sou de Mossoró/RN, cidade com forte tradição no setor de petróleo e gás, e atuo há mais de duas décadas na indústria de energia, com experiência em operações, manutenção e gestão, especialmente em estações de compressão de gás natural e processos operacionais do segmento de óleo e gás.

Ao longo da minha trajetória, acompanhei de perto os desafios e oportunidades da integração entre tecnologia, eficiência operacional e gestão empresarial, e por isso vejo no SAP uma ferramenta estratégica para transformar processos, aumentar produtividade e apoiar decisões no setor energético.

Também sou autor de livros voltados para a área, compartilhando conhecimento prático e técnico com profissionais, estudantes e empresas. Acredito muito no poder da troca de experiências e do aprendizado contínuo.

Estou aqui para aprender com todos vocês, contribuir com minha vivência no segmento de petróleo e gás e ampliar conexões com profissionais que enxergam a tecnologia como motor de crescimento e inovação.

Contem comigo!

entry.title: Re: Implementing Offline HTML Email Approval for PR Workf...
entry.content:
What ECC provides is Extended Notification, which can send emails with links or summaries, but the actual approval still happens inside SAP or through a web interface. So it’s not really offline in the sense you’re describing.

That kind of functionality is available in SAP SRM, where inbound and outbound email processing is part of the design, allowing approvals via email replies. ECC doesn’t include that same framework out of the box.

If you want to achieve something similar in ECC without third-party tools, it becomes a custom solution. Typically this means generating structured HTML emails via SAPconnect, then building inbound email handling to read responses from the mailbox and interpret them as approve or reject actions, and finally updating the workflow work item accordingly. The tricky part is handling parsing reliably and making sure security is covered, since email-based approvals can be spoofed if not designed carefully.

In practice, most teams either stick with Extended Notification and use a web or Fiori inbox for approvals, or accept the effort of a custom build if true email-based approval is a hard requirement.

entry.title: Re: SAP Success factor clock in/out intergrate with facia...
entry.content:
Yes, SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking (Clock In and Clock Out) can be integrated with facial recognition systems. However, this is not available as a standard native feature and requires integration with third-party biometric solutions using APIs.

How the integration works:

The integration typically follows a three-layer architecture:

1. Facial Recognition Device (Biometric System)

Employees authenticate themselves at entry or exit points using facial recognition devices. These devices capture and record time events such as clock-in and clock-out.

2. Middleware Integration Layer

The captured data is then transmitted through an integration layer, which may include SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI). This layer is responsible for transforming the biometric data into a format compatible with SAP SuccessFactors.

3. SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking

The processed data is sent to SAP SuccessFactors using REST-based APIs, such as External Time Data or Time Events APIs. These events are then validated and stored as employee time entries within the system.

Conclusion

SAP supports integration with biometric attendance systems, including facial recognition, through REST APIs for clock-in and clock-out. With the appropriate middleware and configuration, organizations can enable automated and secure attendance tracking.

Thanks & Regards,

Arfat.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build app subscription failed_Trail Account
entry.content:
This kind of “Subscription failed” in SAP Build Apps trial is usually not very descriptive, but in most cases it’s related to missing or incorrect BTP setup rather than the trial itself being broken.

A common cause is that the subaccount is missing proper trust configuration with Identity Authentication (IAS) or OpenID Connect. Without that trust established, the subscription can fail during provisioning even if the booster runs successfully.

Another frequent issue is entitlement or quota mismatch in the trial account. Even if the service is visible in the marketplace, the subaccount may not actually have available Build Apps entitlement assigned, which leads to a silent subscription failure.

It can also happen if the trial account provisioning is partially broken or the booster didn’t fully complete the setup. In that case, removing the failed subscription and re-running the booster sometimes helps, but often a fresh subaccount is needed.

If possible, check the “show details” of the failed subscription and also verify Trust Configuration and Entitlements in the subaccount. If everything looks correct but it still fails, it’s usually something on the trial backend side and worth raising in SAP support/community as a known trial issue.

entry.title: Re: Standard Return Order - Issues
entry.content:
Here’s a clean forum-style reply you can post:

In standard SAP, issues with standard return orders usually come down to configuration or copy control rather than the return process itself.

Most common areas to check are whether the return order type (like RE or your custom return type) has correct copy control from the original document in VTAF, especially item category and pricing type settings. If pricing is not behaving correctly or quantities are not updating, it is often related to pricing type in copy control or item category settings not being set for returns.

Another frequent issue is pricing determination. If the return order is created without reference, the system will try to determine pricing via OVKK. If the correct combination of sales area, customer pricing procedure, and document pricing procedure is missing, the system will fail or pick incorrect pricing conditions. This is a classic cause of return order errors. (SAP Community)

Also check whether the item category in the return order is relevant for pricing and returns processing. If it is not set correctly, you may see wrong net values or missing updates when changing quantities. (SAP Community)

If the issue is related to returns with reference to billing, then pricing type in copy control becomes critical. A wrong pricing type can prevent correct repricing when quantities are changed, which leads to inconsistent values in the return order. (SAP Support Portal)

In most cases, once copy control, pricing type, and OVKK determination are aligned, standard return order processing works without issues.

If you can share the exact error message or scenario (with reference or without reference), it will be easier to pinpoint the root cause.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add a custom field to sap maintenan...
entry.content:
Hello @Lukas_Mx 

Thank you for your question.

The maintenance business objects behind the process may support custom fields, but this does not automatically mean that you can extend SAP Maintenance Assistant with those fields.

The best way to verify this is to open the Custom Fields app, select the relevant maintenance business context, and check the available UIs and Reports or usage options. If you do not find it there, then you cannot add the custom field to the mobile app through standard key user extensibility.

Consult these references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/bee267ff012c4624980fcc71be10d880.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/59bf94a3d1e14526bfc3f28fa0b651ca.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/bb048da3cf164f4c8f98c2f2c9b7caa7.html?locale=en-US

I recommend the following next steps:

  • Identify the exact object where you need the field, for example maintenance order, notification, operation, equipment, or functional location.
  • Use the correct business context to create a test field in the Custom Fields app

  • Check whether SAP Maintenance Assistant is available under the supported UIs or channels.

  • Test the field first in the relevant standard Fiori app, such as Change Maintenance Order or Change Maintenance Notification.
  • If the field works in the backend app but not in SAP Maintenance Assistant, consider raising a case with SAP or suggest improvement for the specific mobile app screen.

Below more reference for more guiding details.

https://help.sap.com/doc/dfe7048eb27445619838125576edd247/2508/en-US/f8e902da2d6840ce83f2e07b2872f712.pdf

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: CL_ABAP_PARALLEL: Parallel Processing Without the aRFC Enable Function Modules
entry.content:
The Problem Every ABAP Developer Knows Too Well

You're running a year-end reconciliation report that loops through 80,000 vendor invoices, validates them against GL postings, and stamps each record with an approval status. In a test system with 500 entries it finishes in under a minute. In production it runs for four hours — and the business is calling.

The knee-jerk fix most developers reach for is raw asynchronous RFC (aRFC): sprinkle CALL FUNCTION … STARTING NEW TASK … DESTINATION IN GROUP, wire up callback handlers, manage semaphore flags, and pray that your RFC server group is configured correctly across all application servers. It works — eventually — but the resulting code is fragile, hard to test, and nearly impossible for the next developer to maintain without a war story attached.

There is a better way: CL_ABAP_PARALLEL.

What Is CL_ABAP_PARALLEL?

CL_ABAP_PARALLEL is a standard SAP class (available from SAP Basis 7.54 and fully released in ABAP Cloud) that wraps the complexity of aRFC-based parallel dispatch behind a clean, object-oriented interface. Instead of orchestrating raw function module calls, RFC groups, and callback patterns yourself, you define what each unit of work should do, hand the instances over to the framework, and let it handle the rest — process allocation, dispatching, timeout management, and result collection.

Under the hood, the framework still uses RS_ABAP_PARALLEL and the server dispatch class CL_SSI_DISPATCH, so the performance characteristics are identical to hand-rolled aRFC. The difference is in developer experience and maintainability.

Two Ways to Implement: Choose Your Pattern

SAP provides two distinct implementation strategies. Pick the one that fits your team's style and the complexity of your data contracts.

Pattern 1 — Interface Implementation (IF_ABAP_PARALLEL)

This is the recommended modern approach, particularly for ABAP Cloud and clean-core projects. Your processing class implements the IF_ABAP_PARALLEL interface and carries its own typed input data as instance attributes. No serialization gymnastics required.

"---------------------------------------------------------------------
" Step 1: Define the processing task class
"---------------------------------------------------------------------
CLASS zcl_invoice_validation_task DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_abap_parallel.
    INTERFACES if_serializable_object.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_result,
             invoice_id  TYPE vbeln,
             is_valid    TYPE abap_bool,
             error_msg   TYPE string,
           END OF ty_result.

    METHODS constructor
      IMPORTING
        is_invoice TYPE rbkp.           " MIRO invoice header

    METHODS get_result
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_result) TYPE ty_result.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA ms_invoice TYPE rbkp.
    DATA ms_result  TYPE ty_result.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_invoice_validation_task IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD constructor.
    ms_invoice = is_invoice.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_abap_parallel~do.
    " All validation logic lives here — runs in a separate dialog WP
    ms_result-invoice_id = ms_invoice-belnr.

    " Example: Cross-check the invoice amount against the PO tolerance
    DATA(lv_tolerance) = CONV dec15_5( '0.05' ).   " 5 % tolerance
    DATA lv_po_net TYPE wrbtr.

    SELECT SINGLE netwr INTO lv_po_net
      FROM ekko
      WHERE ebeln = ms_invoice-xblnr.  " vendor doc nr maps to PO

    IF lv_po_net IS NOT INITIAL.
      DATA(lv_deviation) = abs( ms_invoice-rwbtr - lv_po_net ) / lv_po_net.
      IF lv_deviation <= lv_tolerance.
        ms_result-is_valid = abap_true.
      ELSE.
        ms_result-is_valid  = abap_false.
        ms_result-error_msg = |Deviation { lv_deviation * 100 } % exceeds tolerance|.
      ENDIF.
    ELSE.
      ms_result-is_valid  = abap_false.
      ms_result-error_msg = 'No matching purchase order found'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_result.
    rs_result = ms_result.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.


"---------------------------------------------------------------------
" Step 2: Orchestrate the parallel run from your main program / method
"---------------------------------------------------------------------
CLASS zcl_invoice_processor DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS run_validation.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_invoice_processor IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD run_validation.

    " Fetch the raw invoice headers to validate
    DATA lt_invoices TYPE TABLE OF rbkp.
    SELECT * FROM rbkp
      INTO TABLE @LT_invoices
      WHERE bukrs = '1000'
        AND bldat >= @( cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) - 30 ).

    " Build one task object per invoice
    DATA lt_tasks TYPE cl_abap_parallel=>t_in_inst_tab.
    LOOP AT lt_invoices ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<invoice>).
      DATA(lo_task) = NEW zcl_invoice_validation_task( <invoice> ).
      APPEND lo_task TO lt_tasks.
    ENDLOOP.

    " Instantiate the framework — use 40 % of available dialog processes
    DATA(lo_parallel) = NEW cl_abap_parallel(
      p_percentage = 40
      p_timeout    = 300   " seconds per task
    ).

    " Fire!
    DATA lt_results TYPE cl_abap_parallel=>t_out_inst_tab.
    lo_parallel->run_inst(
      EXPORTING p_in_tab  = lt_tasks
      IMPORTING p_out_tab = lt_results
    ).

    " Harvest results
    LOOP AT lt_results ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<res>).

      " Check for framework-level errors first (timeouts, system failures)
      IF <res>-message IS NOT INITIAL.
        MESSAGE <res>-message TYPE 'W'.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.

      " Cast the generic reference back to our concrete task class
      DATA(lo_finished_task) = CAST zcl_invoice_validation_task( <res>-instance ).
      DATA(ls_val_result)    = lo_finished_task->get_result( ).

      IF ls_val_result-is_valid = abap_false.
        " Write rejection log, trigger workflow, etc.
        cl_demo_output=>write( |Invoice { ls_val_result-invoice_id }: { ls_val_result-error_msg }| ).
      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

The key advantage here is type safety. Your invoice data lives in a properly typed class attribute (ms_invoice TYPE rbkp) — no intermediate XSTRING packing or XML transformation. When you cast <res>-instance back to zcl_invoice_validation_task, you get full IntelliSense and compile-time checks in ADT.

Pattern 2 — Inheritance from CL_ABAP_PARALLEL (Classic Style)

In this variant your task class inherits from CL_ABAP_PARALLEL and redefines the DO method. Input and output are exchanged via the inherited P_IN / P_OUT XSTRING parameters, which you serialize and deserialize yourself using EXPORT … TO DATA BUFFER / IMPORT … FROM DATA BUFFER.

This pattern is occasionally useful when working on older Basis releases or when you need to carry a heterogeneous shared payload (P_IN_ALL) across all tasks without repeating it for every record.

CLASS zcl_material_enrichment DEFINITION
  PUBLIC INHERITING FROM cl_abap_parallel
  FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS do REDEFINITION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_material_enrichment IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD do.
    " Deserialize per-task input
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_input,
             matnr TYPE matnr,
             werks TYPE werks_d,
           END OF ty_input.
    DATA ls_input TYPE ty_input.
    IMPORT task_data = ls_input FROM DATA BUFFER p_in.

    " Do the heavy lifting — e.g. call an external pricing API
    DATA lv_price TYPE p LENGTH 10 DECIMALS 2.
    " ... your logic here ...

    " Serialize the result back
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_output,
             matnr TYPE matnr,
             price TYPE p LENGTH 10 DECIMALS 2,
           END OF ty_output.
    DATA ls_output TYPE ty_output.
    ls_output-matnr = ls_input-matnr.
    ls_output-price = lv_price.
    EXPORT result_data = ls_output TO DATA BUFFER p_out.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Practical tip: Prefer Pattern 1 (interface) for all new development. Reserve Pattern 2 for legacy codebases where you need XSTRING-based data exchange with an existing framework or where shared read-only context (P_IN_ALL) can meaningfully reduce payload size.

Constructor Parameters — What They Mean and How They Interact

One of the most common sources of confusion with CL_ABAP_PARALLEL is the interplay between its four resource-control parameters. Here is a clear breakdown:

Parameter Type Effect
P_PERCENTAGEI (0–100)Use X % of the dialog processes that the system deems available at startup. Takes precedence over P_NUM_TASKS if both are supplied.
P_NUM_TASKSIRequest a fixed number of parallel slots. Ignored if P_PERCENTAGE is also set or if the value exceeds the system maximum.
P_NUM_PROCESSESIHard cap on the total simultaneous processes across all app servers. A safeguard against monopolizing the system.
P_TIMEOUTI (seconds)Maximum wall-clock time a single task may run before the framework marks it as failed and populates <res>-message.
P_LOCAL_SERVERFlagWhen set, all tasks run on the same application server as the calling program. Useful for debugging; avoid in production.

Error Handling You Cannot Ignore

Every entry in p_out_tab (or p_out_inst_tab) has a MESSAGE field. This is not optional to check. If a task times out, encounters a system failure, or the framework cannot dispatch it, the result record will have a non-initial MESSAGE and a blank result. Silently skipping that check means silently dropping data.

LOOP AT lt_results ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<r>).
  IF <r>-message IS NOT INITIAL.
    " Log to application log (SLG1) instead of just writing to screen
    cl_bali_log=>...   " or your preferred logging utility
    CONTINUE.
  ENDIF.
  " ... process normal result ...
ENDLOOP.

Additionally, wrap your entire run_inst call in a TRY … CATCH block for cx_sy_parallel_processing to handle catastrophic dispatch failures gracefully.

Quick Comparison: Before and After

Aspect Raw aRFC Pattern CL_ABAP_PARALLEL
BoilerplateRFC function module + callback handler + semaphore flagsOne class implementing IF_ABAP_PARALLEL
Type safetyEXPORT/IMPORT tables, genericTyped class attributes (Pattern 1)
Error handlingManual SYSTEM-EXCEPTIONS per callBuilt-in MESSAGE field per result
TestabilityDifficult (requires RFC infrastructure)Standard class — full unit-test coverage possible
ABAP Cloud compatibleNo (aRFC not available in Cloud)Yes (CL_ABAP_PARALLEL is released)

Closing Thoughts

CL_ABAP_PARALLEL does not do anything fundamentally new — parallel dialog dispatch has been in ABAP for decades. What it does is remove all the ceremony that made developers avoid it. With the interface-based pattern, adding parallelism to an existing data-processing class is often a matter of implementing one interface, one constructor, and adjusting a handful of lines in the orchestrating method.

If your team has been avoiding parallel processing because the old aRFC boilerplate felt too risky or too expensive to maintain, it is time to revisit that decision. Your business users — and the developers who come after you — will thank you.

entry.title: Re: SFM CCF - Dynamically distribute emissions to product...
entry.content:
Hello Paul_H,

Thank you for detailing your setup so clearly! The behavior and the errors you are experiencing are actually expected and align with the standard design of the SFM calculation engine.

To answer your main question directly: Machine/Resource times from Internal Service Confirmations are not enough on their own for the system to distribute the Total Energy Consumption.

Here is the technical logic behind this: While the Internal Service Confirmations provide the duration of usage (how many hours a machine ran), the calculation engine still needs to understand the energy intensity of each specific resource. For example, the system cannot assume that a heavy industrial machine and a low-voltage assembly line consume the same amount of energy per hour.

To solve this, the engine requires the Planned Energy Consumption Rates. The system uses these planned rates as a baseline, multiplies them by the real work times, and derives an accurate distribution factor. This weighting factor is what allows the total energy bill (or macro meter reading) to be dynamically and proportionally distributed among all resources month over month.

How to resolve this: You have two architectural paths according to the official standard design:

  1. Use Planned Rates (Recommended for your setup): You need to configure the Manage Planned Energy Consumption Rates app for the 4 resources showing errors. This is usually a one-time setup. Once entered, the system will use them alongside your actual machine hours to dynamically distribute the actual emissions to the product level without fixed allocation ratios.

  2. Energy Flow Model Using Only Meters: The only way to completely skip Planned Rates is if you have individual sub-meters physically installed and configured for each of your production resources in the Energy Flow Model, feeding actual data into the GHG Data Form for every single machine.

For further learning on how these calculation methods and distribution factors are structured, please refer to the official SAP Help documentation:

  • Overview of all calculation scenarios: Energy Distribution Scenarios

  • How distribution factors are calculated specifically for your scenario: Energy Flow Model Using Only Planned Energy Consumption Rates

I hope this helps clarify the calculation logic and helps you clear those errors!

Best regards,

Kauê Da Hora 
SAP Technical Support PC ERP (SCT/SFM)

entry.title: Sales Order creation via EDI using API - New Customer Part creates useless TATX line.
entry.content:
We are implementing S4/HANA on private cloud.

We are loading customer standard order from EDI using the API.

Several customer only order using the buyer part number.

The issue is when the customer orders a new part where we haven't set up a CMIR the line loads as a TATX line with the new buyer part in the Customer Material field.

OK, but this is next to useless. Sure we can figure out what they are ordering and set up a CMIR but then what?

The line has no quantity so the CSR doesn't know HOW much they ordered.

The CSR has no way to reprocess the order to the item becomes a normal item.

We have no idea what the next step is for this kind of error.

entry.title: Re: Getting Error: Parameterized views can't be used with...
entry.content:
Hello, the JSON should look like this: 

 

// Default (local development) — use SQLite
"requires": {
"db": {
"kind": "sqlite",
"credentials": { "database": ":memory:" }
}
},

// Hybrid profile — use HANA with explicit dialect
"[hybrid]": {
"requires": {
"db": { "kind": "hana" }
},
"sql": {
"dialect": "hana" // ← THIS IS THE CRITICAL MISSING LINE
}
},

// Production profile — use HANA with explicit dialect
"[production]": {
"requires": {
"db": { "kind": "hana" }
},
"sql": {
"dialect": "hana" // ← Required here too
}
}
}

entry.title: Data Model EHS Classic
entry.content:
Introduction

EHS Classic allows lot of options to manage data. We have data elements like

  • identifier
  • Specification header
  • Specific topics as Reference / Inheritance
  • Component type / exception values
  • Phrase based characteristics
  • Regulatory LIst based characteristics
  • Other types of characteristics

and much more

Depending on you starting point: there are some rules you should use to avoid a crash of your data model.

Assumption is now: you use 3E / SAP Standard model

Hints of topics to avoid

  • For phrase based characteristic: do not add customer specific phrases to the phrase set; only if you are sure that the characteristic is not used by a 3E rule set: you should do a mix of 3E and customer specific phrases
  • Do not add e.g. own compontent types / exception values or own Regulatory lists as long as you now: what is 3E Standard and what is the reason to add something new
  • Do never manipulate SAP EHS classes/characteristics
  • If needed: always "copy" the SAP class to a customer specific one
  • Keep in mind the VERY important topic of "Unit of Measurement"; if you are not taking care here: you will get a mess
  • You should only carefully adapt the 3E original WWI templates to your need and you should have a good documentation what was changed and you need potentially a strategy: hwo to make updates whicih happens in 3E WWI templates and copy them to your customer specific one
  • You should use a "most primitive set up" of your system. There are so many options to enhance the system; at some point in time you might not have the overview what was changed

What you can do with low risk

  • prepare own new value assignment tyes plus if needed classes/characteritics
  • prepare own property tree
  • Define whatever kind of strategy for using the "Substance Authority group"
  • If you would like to use your customer specific topics in a customer specific WWI template: yo can do so

For Superusers

If you have understood all the options provided by SAP you can do a clever set up to support the maintenance in many places. But you need to check: what option is the best. As one example: you can prepare a "dyanmic phrase set" strategy; but there is much more you can use if needed.

And if you are the "super super" user: you can completely change look and feel in CG02/CG02BD.

entry.title: Lessons from the field: What Early Adopters of SAP IBP’s Harmonized Planning Area learned
entry.content:
In September 2025, SAP launched an Early Adopter Care (EAC) program for customers implementing the Harmonized Planning Area (HPA) in SAP IBP. Over 7+ months, 15+ companies across industries joined weekly knowledge-exchange sessions with SAP Product Management, Development, and expert consultants — covering integration architecture, data model deep-dives, and go-live preparation. This blog distills those lessons for the benefit of a wider IBP user community. 

 The Participants — A diverse cross-industry group 

The program attracted 15+ companies from Automotive, Food & Beverage, Life Sciences, Chemicals, High Tech, Consumer Goods, and Agriculture — spanning EMEA and Americas, from mid-size players to global multinationals. Planning scope ranged from a single module to five deployed simultaneously. Integration strategies spanned the full spectrum: RTI-only, CI-only and hybrid. 

Why the Participants joined the initiative: 

Participants joined the HPA EAC for different reasons, but several themes emerged repeatedly: 

  • Greenfield HPA: Starting fresh on IBP and choosing HPA from day one rather than implementing a legacy planning area. 
  • New business unit rollout: Companies already on SAPIBP1 piloting HPA in a second business unit before broader migration. 
  • APO replacement: Early access to understand gaps and plan the transition away from SAP APO. 
  • Exclusive capabilities: Features like harmonized scenarios, vendor-managed inventory exist only in HPA — no alternative for companies with these requirements. 
  • Product influence: A direct channel to SAP Product Management to raise requirements and shape the roadmap while HPA was still maturing.  

Integration decisions — RTI, CI, or both? 

One of the most impactful decisions every HPA customer faces is their integration strategy. The EAC revealed that there is no one-size-fits-all answer — and that most customers end up with a hybrid approach, at least initially. 

The Three Options: 

Technology 

Best for 

Limitations 

RTI (Real-Time Integration) 

Standard master data, OBP transactions, vendor data, delta integration 

No public cloud support; limited to SAP systems; plant-independent requirements not supported 

CI (Cloud Integration) 

Multi-ERP landscapes, non-SAP systems, flexible transformations, public cloud 

Application job template integration was not available during the EAC program 

CIDS (Cloud Integration Data Services) 

Existing implementations, custom enrichment, backward compatibility 

Legacy technology; no longer bundled for new customers; limited tracing 

 

Case Study 1: The CI-Only Approach: A customer made the bold decision to use Cloud Integration exclusively — no RTI at all. Their rationale: 

  • RTI does not work with public cloud deployments or non-SAP ERP systems 
  • They integrate with 4+ different ERP systems globally — RTI only connects to SAP 
  • Internal expertise for CI was more available than for RTI 
  • CI enables trial operations before full S/4HANA migration 

Their architecture: Custom CDS views in S/4HANA handles all data transformation. CI flows are kept “dumb” — pure data transportation with no business logic. This separation of concerns makes debugging and maintenance straightforward. 

Case Study 2: The Hybrid Approach: This customer uses RTI for base master data integration (the standard objects that RTI handles well) and enriches with CIDS for additional attributes that require custom logic on the S/4HANA side. This is a pragmatic approach when you need RTI’s delta capabilities but also have custom fields that RTI bodies don’t cover. 

Key learning — CI vs. CIDS: One customer who migrated from CIDS to SAP CI was really happy with migration. They cited better flexibility, superior tracing, no data agent performance issues, and easier team collaboration since CI skills are not limited to IBP specialists. 

RTI recommendation from SAP: SAP’s integration strategy recommendation for new customers is to go RTI first. This gives customers delta integration capabilities which is not available with time-series methods. RTI also future-proofs the integration — if customer starts with time series and later adopts OBP, the RTI integration is already available. 

Value realization: 

Despite being early adopters on a maturing platform, participants reported concrete value across several dimensions: 

First Customer Live on HPA: In March 2026, a participant became one of the first customers to go live on the Harmonized Planning Area — with SOP, Inventory Optimization, and Time Series Optimizer all active simultaneously. Some of the key values realized by these early adopters are: 

  • Configuration simplification: Customers discovered that with introduction of IBP_CONVERSION function, HPA eliminates the need of helper key figures and additional planning levels for UOM conversion. The system converts automatically without any helpers KFs. Pre-configured conversions cover most use cases. 
  • Unified planning model: Participants who activated both time-series and order-based planning in a single HPA confirmed the core value proposition: consistent results across both planning methods, unified key figures, and no reconciliation required between separate planning areas. One participant preparing for a June 2026 go-live is running TS + OBP + Inventory Optimization + S&OP + Demand & Response Planning — all in one planning area. 

Challenges and concerns raised 

The EAC program is valuable precisely because participants surface real challenges — not just success stories. Here are the most significant concerns raised, presented honestly for the benefit of future adopters. 

Some of the key concerns raised by participants: 

  • Subnetwork filtering in TS optimizer: One consumer goods company cannot limit the time-series optimizer to a specific factory — it runs across the entire global supply chain. Without subnetwork filtering (planned for 2611), planners cannot run optimization at their scope.  
  • Cross-location vendor mapping: An automotive company operating in 60–70 countries discovered that RTI’s inbound mapping allows only one vendor code per source location. With multiple subsidiaries ordering from the same distribution center using different vendor codes, they cannot build their supply network.  
  • Scheduling agreements as source of supply: Critical for automotive planning but not available in HPA until 2608. 

 How SAP supported EAC participants:  

One of the defining characteristics of the EAC format is that customer challenges are shared directly with the product managers and developers who can act on them. SAP team responded to customer concerns by updating product roadmap. Some features committed to Roadmap based on customer inputs are: 

Customer needs

SAP response 

Planned release 

Subnetwork filtering in TS optimizer 

Confirmed on roadmap 

IBP 2611 

Scheduling agreements for Response & Supply 

Confirmed on roadmap 

IBP 2608 

Cross-location vendor mapping in RTI 

Roadmap to extend mapping table with role and location info 

2605–2608 

Look-up logic for calendars/attributes 

Delivered 

IBP 2602 

Receiving calendar hierarchy inheritance 

Part of hierarchical lookup feature 

IBP 2602 

 Migration Support announced:  SAP7F Migration EAC: A dedicated EAC program for brownfield customers migrating from SAP7F to HPA, is planned to start with the 2605 release.  If you are interested, please register with following link : Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence

Best Practice content delivered: In direct response to EAC participant requests, SAP delivered executable best practice content in IBP 2511 — including planning views, analytic stories, and planning profiles that customers could use immediately rather than building from scratch. 

Real-Time issue resolution: Pattern observed throughout the EAC: When customers raised specific technical issues (e.g., attribute naming bugs, OKF behavior questions, lot-for-lot procedure gaps), SAP development teams often investigated and responded within 1–2 sessions — far faster than the standard support ticket process. 

Looking forward — Recommendations for future adopters 

Drawing on seven months of EAC discussions, here are the distilled recommendations for customers considering HPA adoption: 

  1. Start small with partial-scope copy : You do not need to adopt every HPA module at once. When copying I_SAPIBP2, select only what you need — Demand Planning and IO is a common starting point. Expand your footprint as you gain confidence. 
  2. Choose your integration strategy early: The RTI vs. CI decision has cascading effects on your architecture, team skills, and timeline.  
  3. Leverage coexistence for phased transition: HPA and SAP7F planning areas can run side-by-side on the same tenant using separate logical systems. This means you can validate HPA in a contained scope before decommissioning legacy areas — reducing risk significantly. 
  4. Plan for the upgrade lifecycle from day one: When you copy I_SAPIBP2, keep the original sample planning area untouched as a reference for future comparison. When new releases deliver additional content, use the planning area comparison tool to identify deltas and merge selectively. 
  5. Engage with SAP early: Customers in the EAC consistently received faster issue resolution, influenced the product roadmap directly, and benefited from peer learning that would have taken months to discover independently. If an EAC program exists for a capability you’re adopting, join it. 
  6. Best practice content Is your accelerator: Since IBP 2511, SAP has delivered executable planning views, analytic stories, and job profiles for HPA. Use them as your starting point rather than building from scratch. EAC participants who used the pre-release content reported significant time savings in their setup. 

Summary: 

 HPA is where SAP IBP is heading, and the early adopters in this program showed the way. They validated that the model works, the integration options are viable, and the value proposition is real — leaving a clear path for those who follow.

entry.title: How to Prepare a Successful OpenText VIM Landscape Upgrade
entry.content:
• 1. What Is OpenText VIM Versioning?
  • 2. Compatibility Check with SAP S/4HANA release version
  • 3. OCR / Capture Component Compatibility Check
  • 4.Validation Client and the Shift to Fiori App Capture Validation Workplace
  • 5. OpenText Archive Center and VIM Upgrade
  • 6. VIM Foundation and Invoice Solution Upgrade (excluding Beyond Invoice component)
  • 7. Concluded VIM Upgrade Preparation Framework

1. What Is OpenText VIM Versioning?

OpenText VIM versioning follows release numbers such as 7.6, 20.4, 23.4, 24.4, and 25.4. These versions are not only product labels. They indicate which SAP platform versions, add-ons, Fiori components, capture integrations, and support package levels are expected to work together.


A simple way to understand VIM versioning is:

VIM generationTypical SAP platform relationshipGeneral meaning
VIM 7.6SAP ERP / ECC-oriented landscapesOlder generation, still relevant in legacy ERP environments
VIM 20.4Earlier SAP S/4HANA releasesOften used in S/4HANA 2020–2022 landscapes
VIM 23.4S/4HANA 2023 generationTransitional release with more modern Fiori and Foundation capabilities
VIM 25.4S/4HANA 2025 generationTarget release for latest S/4HANA 2025 compatibility and newer functions


This is the most important message for beginners: VIM versioning must be read together with the SAP S/4HANA version. You cannot safely decide the VIM target version without first confirming the SAP target version.

2. Compatibility Check with SAP S/4HANA release version 

The first step in any VIM upgrade preparation is to confirm the target SAP S/4HANA release.

This is important because VIM is installed as an SAP system add-on, and each VIM version supports specific SAP S/4HANA releases. Therefore, the VIM target version should be decided based on the SAP target release, not independently.

For example, if the SAP system is being upgraded to SAP S/4HANA 2025, the corresponding VIM version must be 25.4, according to SAP Note 3654390: https://me.sap.com/notes/3654390.

If the SAP system remains on an older S/4HANA release, the latest VIM version may not be compatible.

3. OCR / Capture Component Compatibility Check

The OCR and capture area can be confusing because OpenText product names have changed across generations.

Before upgrading, do not rely only on the name used by the customer.

TopicComponent / NameWhat it meansUpgrade attention point
BCCBCCOlder Business Center Capture generation used in legacy ECC / VIM landscapes.VIM 25.4 does not supported any BCC/ICC components anymore
IES (Information Extraction Services)IC4S / C4SOn-premise Capture for SAP Solutions based on IES. IC4S and C4S refer to the same product line across naming changes.Check Windows Server, IIS, .NET, learning data, capture profiles, and Validation Client compatibility.
Core Capture / CC4SCloud-based IES capture optionCheck cloud readiness
Supporting Plug-InCapture Imaging Plug-InSupporting imaging component for capture/rendering. 

Always check the installed product, version, deployment model, and compatibility with the target VIM Foundation version.

4. Validation Client and the Shift to Fiori App Capture Validation Workplace

The Validation Client is used to review and correct OCR extraction results before documents continue in the VIM process.

For upgrade preparation, the key rule is simple: keep the Validation Client on the same release level as VIM Foundation and the OCR/Capture component. 

As an alternative, customers can also use the Fiori Capture Validation Workplace for browser-based validation. This reduces dependency on local Windows client installation and aligns validation with the modern Fiori user experience.

In short: align the Validation Client version with VIM and Capture, or consider Fiori validation as the modern alternative.

5. OpenText Archive Center and VIM Upgrade

OpenText Archive Center, or OTAC, is used to store invoice images and attachments.

VIM does not store the physical document itself; VIM manages the invoice process, while the archive repository stores the document content. The connection is usually handled through SAP ArchiveLink:

ComponentRole
VIMManages invoice metadata, workflow, business rules, approval, and posting.
OTAC / Archive RepositoryStores and retrieves invoice images and attachments.
SAP ArchiveLinkLinks the SAP/VIM document record to the stored document image.

For upgrade preparation, there is generally no direct version compatibility dependency between VIM and OTAC.

6. VIM Foundation and Invoice Solution Upgrade (excluding Beyond Invoice component) 

For VIM upgrade preparation, the key point is to identify which SAP add-ons are installed today and which add-ons are required for the target VIM version.

ComponentExample add-on name in VIM 25.4Role
VIM FoundationOTVIMBASBase framework for inbound processing, capture integration, workflow, and Fiori foundation.
VIM Invoice SolutionOTVIMINVInvoice-specific processing, including PO/non-PO invoices, business rules, approvals, exceptions, and posting.
VIM Fiori UIOTVIMFUIProvides Fiori apps and UI framework for VIM users.
Optional scenario add-onsFor example, Ariba or TM-related VIM add-onsSupports additional integration scenarios such as Ariba CIG or Transportation Management.

SAINT (SAP Transaction): VIM add-ons are usually installed or upgraded by the SAP Basis team using SAINT, the SAP Add-On Installation Tool. 

7. Concluded VIM Upgrade Preparation Framework

Use below checklist as a VIM upgrade preparation framework:

Use this order as the upgrade readiness checklist:

  1. SAP S/4HANA target release
    Which SAP S/4HANA version will be used?
  2. VIM target version
    Which VIM Foundation and VIM Invoice Solution version is required?
  3. Capture / OCR model
    Will capture stay on-premise, or move to cloud capture? What OCR solution version will be adopted?
  4. Validation approach
    Will users validate invoices in Windows Validation Client, Fiori Validation Workplace, or both?
  5. Fiori deployment model
    Will VIM Fiori apps be deployed on-premise or via SAP BTP?
  6. Archive repository
    Which archive repository and ArchiveLink setup will be used?
  7. Language and country scope
    Which languages and countries are in scope for AP users, approvers, and validators?
  8. Integration scope
    Which integrations must remain active, such as email, scan, IDoc, Ariba, TM, DRC, or custom APIs?

 

Good luck to have a smooth and successful OpenText VIM upgrade.

Read more of my article on VIM introduction: VIM Foundation & Invoice Solution Configuration Ov... - SAP Community

entry.title: Salary from start date of Bonus Plan
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I have a scenario where I need to consider the salary of eligible employees starting from the plan’s start date. For example, I have a bonus plan that runs for three years (2024–2026). In this case, I need to use the salary effective as of January 1, 2024, along with the target percentage from that same date, rather than using values as of December 31, 2026. Can this be achieved using point‑in‑time functionality? If not, what alternative approach would you suggest? 

@DavidSomelofske  @xavierlegarrec @johnking : Looking forward for your suggestion. 

Thanks 

Ritu

entry.title: SAP career transition guidance
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
I am exploring a career transition into SAP consulting and would greatly appreciate insights from experienced professionals and consultants in this forum.
A brief background about myself:
30 years old | CMA Inter | 7 years of experience in taxation within manufacturing MNCs
Work spans corporate tax compliance, tax reporting, and related areas across multiple jurisdictions
Active SAP end user throughout my career, working regularly with finance and tax-related modules
I have the following questions specifically around SAP PAPM (Profitability and Performance Management):
Is transitioning into SAP PAPM consulting advisable at my current stage, given my age and finance/tax background?
What does a realistic career trajectory in SAP PAPM consulting look like for someone from a tax/finance domain?
What roadmap would you recommend — certification sequence, practical exposure, and positioning for consulting roles?
How does the SAP PAPM job market look over the next 10–15 years, given S/4HANA adoption and the shift to cloud?
Are there realistic opportunities for onsite roles or international postings within SAP PAPM consulting?
How does SAP PAPM complement other SAP finance modules, and is it a strong standalone specialisation?
I understand everyone here is busy, and even a brief response or a pointer in the right direction would be truly helpful.
Thank you in advance for your time and guidance.

entry.title: The condition class in S4 HANA is not created BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 but it is in R3
entry.content:
Good morning everyone.
Currently, my client is in the process of migrating to S/4HANA. We have a program Z that creates sales orders (VA01) through the BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2. The program fills in all the required fields of the BAPI (it enters four condition classes per item), but after creating the order, it does so without the ZCGM condition, which is manually configured as mandatory for a specific order class, ZC06. If I create the order manually using VA01, SAP prompts me to enter the ZCGM condition class since it is mandatory, and it creates the other classes without problems.

The curious thing here is that this same BAPI is used in R/3, and the same fields are filled in, but the order is created using the ZCGM condition class.

I have seen that at the configuration level, everything is OK in both R/3 and S/4HANA. I performed standard debugging on the BAPI and verified that the condition is never lost, but for some reason, it's not created in S4 HANA when other condition classes are added. If only the ZCGM class is used, it creates it without problems, but the client needs to add other condition classes as well.

S4 HANA order created through BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2  (The ZCGM condition is not present.)

Order created in R3 with the same data and using BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2( if created with condition ZCGM )

 

Data that I enter in conditions for the BAPI in both S4 HANA and R3 environments.

We have also tried setting a value in LOGICAL_SWITCH-PRICING but it doesn't work for us in S4 HANA either.

entry.title: Re: Supplier quotation creation using BAPI or FM in S4 HA...
entry.content:
Hi Sudheer,

Thanks for your valuable suggestions. I achieved the Supplier Quotation creation using following steps.
     Main API is API_QTN_PROCESS_SRV.
    1.Create supplier Quoation by calling API : API_QTN_PROCESS_SRV/CreateFromRFQ?QuotationSubmissionDate=datetime{ <lv_date>}Supplier=<lv_lifnr>&RequestForQuotation=<lv_rfq_no>.  

     Set up Http post method for above url.
      Once get the http status code 200 or 201, add the Supplier quotation line item with Quantity and Price using below API.
      API_QTN_PROCESS_SRV/A_SupplierQuotationItem(SupplierQuotation=<lv_quot_no>,SupplierQuotationItem=<lv_item_no>.  This is PATCH method
     After add the all line items, the Quotation will be submitted using following API.
     API_QTN_PROCESS_SRV/Submit?SupplierQuotation<lv_quot_no>.  - This is also Https post method.

Thanks,
Venkat.

entry.title: Re: SF Learning: Online Session timeout
entry.content:
Hello.
Please note that an enhancement request has been submitted regarding this: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#idea/263029

Also, within the LMS system configuration under AUTHENTICATION you can change the value of the session time out. We changed ours to 14400 (seconds) to match the 4 hour timeout that our RISE content is set at on the content server.

# The value of HTTPSessionTimeout is passed to HTTPSession.setMaxInactiveInterval() each time a session is created.
# As of Servlets 2.2-2.5, that implementation accepts values specified in seconds. For example: 1800 is 30 minutes.
# This overrides the value specified in web.xml for the session timeout (in the session-config/session-timeout element).
# The minimum value for httpSessionTimeout is 1800 seconds, and the upper limit is 28800 seconds.
httpSessionTimeout=14400

Also, within LMS system configuration under LMS_ADMIN please ensure you have your keep alive interval set to a lower rate to allow more pings to be sent to platform to keep the session "alive".

# The Online Content Structure page has a LMSsession keep alive feature that is based on this amount of time (in seconds).
# Default: 300 (5 min)
# 0 - Session timeout-600 (i.e. 10 minutes based on the 30 min session time out); Minimum : 120 (i.e. 2 minutes)
contentStructurePageKeepAliveInterval=250
# This is an opt in setting to extend the User session to 4 hours while taking the AICC content.
# It will reset back to normal however when user navigates back to the home page, i.e. away from the content launching page.
extendAiccOnlineSession=true

I hope this helps.

Tom
